journal_entry.description: Discounted 90-day promissory note at bank (collateralized borrowing)
journal_entry.rationale: Since management determined this is a collateralized borrowing (risks and rewards not transferred), the note receivable remains on Ford's books and the bank advance is recorded as a liability. The discount charge of $1,667 ($100,000 × 15% × 40/365 days remaining) is recorded as interest expense. Cash received is $98,333 ($100,000 face value less $1,667 discount).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Collateralized borrowing treatment: note receivable stays on books, bank advance recorded as liability. Cash increases (asset), interest expense increases (expense), and bank loan increases (liability). Discount calculated as $100,000 × 15% × (40 days remaining / 365 days) = $1,666.67. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,666.67 = $98,333.33.
